title: Eva OP Song Wins JASRAC Award
display_title: Eva OP Song Wins JASRAC Award
wikitext:
[]: Eva OP Song Wins JASRAC Award             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Wed May 25, 2011 2:33 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2011-05-25/songs-from-evangelion-other-anime-win-jasrac-awards It's official.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed May 25, 2011 2:37 pm Good.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 25, 2011 4:59 pm Awesome. :)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed May 25, 2011 8:36 pm The song earned the gold prize for its continued royalties from online sales, pachinko and pachislo (pachinko parlor slot machine) games, karaoke, and other venues in 2010 — 15 years after its debut. PACHINKO is a very major source of revenue. The EVA pachinko was extremely popular and kept the anime on mass awareness for years. Royalty of songs was just a tiny part of the income generated. Now you can see how rich Anno and his company (Khara) have become. And Cruel Angels' Theses has been on most popular karaoke song lists for so many years. Many non-anime-fans know this song and this is one song that if you sing it nobody would suspect you are an otaku. When I go to karaoke in Japan, the song almost always come up in "songs that are sung most often this month" list (yes we can check things like that online in karaoke in Japan) JASRAC also handed out an International Award for the music that earned the most money overseas last year. Nine of the top 10 Japanese musical pieces in overseas royalties came from anime Another most interesting piece of news.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu May 26, 2011 6:53 pm symbv wrote: Now you can see how rich Anno and his company (Khara) have become.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu May 26, 2011 7:26 pm Xard wrote: I always wonder if Anno's fortune is counted in millions or not
